blog_posts.title: Best Foundations for Oily Skin That Last All Day
blog_posts.excerpt: Struggling with shine and sliding makeup? Discover pro-approved foundations for oily skin that stay put from morning to night.
blog_posts.content:
If you have oily or combination skin, you know the struggle: your foundation looks flawless at 8 AM and like a slip-and-slide by lunchtime. As a working makeup artist, I test every base product in real-world conditions—warm studios, outdoor weddings, and long photoshoot days.

In this guide, we’ll cover:

1. How to prep oily skin so your foundation adheres properly.
2. Key ingredients to look for (and avoid) when choosing foundation.
3. My favorite long-wear, oil-controlling foundations at different price points.
4. Application tips that prevent caking and oxidation.

### 1. Skin prep for oily skin
Start with a gentle, non-stripping cleanser, followed by a lightweight, oil-free moisturizer. Avoid heavy creams on the T-zone. A silicone-based mattifying primer can help blur pores and control shine...

### 2. Ingredients that help oily skin
Look for terms like "oil-free", "long-wear", and "matte" or "natural matte" finish. Niacinamide and salicylic acid can help regulate oil production over time...

### 3. Pro-approved foundations for oily skin
- Long-Wear Matte: Great for all-day office wear and summer weddings.
- Soft-Matte Serum: Ideal if you want a skin-like finish but still need control in the T-zone.
- Airbrush Formula: Best for special occasions when you’ll be photographed.

### 4. Application & touch-up tricks
Apply foundation in thin layers with a damp sponge, then press a translucent powder into the areas you get most shiny. Carry blotting papers for touch-ups instead of piling on more powder.

Save this article to your reading list so you can revisit it before your next appointment.

blog_posts.title: How to Make Red Lipstick Last All Night
blog_posts.excerpt: From lining to layering, here’s exactly how I prep and apply bold red lipstick for parties and evening events.
blog_posts.content:
A bold red lip is timeless—and unforgiving if it smudges. For evening makeup and party looks, longevity is everything. In this post I break down my full process for getting red lipstick to last through dinner, drinks, and dancing.

We’ll cover:
- Lip prep so color goes on smooth, not patchy.
- Choosing undertones that flatter your skin tone.
- My exact application steps used on clients.
- How to touch up without creating a heavy ring of product.

### 1. Prep
Exfoliate gently, moisturize, then blot away excess balm. Any slip on the lips will shorten wear time...

### 2. Line & layer
I always start with a long-wear liner, then press the first layer of lipstick in with a brush, blot, and repeat.

This technique is used in many of the 2024 red-lip looks featured in my evening portfolio.

blog_posts.title: Bridal Makeup Timeline: When to Book Your Trial
blog_posts.excerpt: Not sure when to schedule your bridal trial? Here’s a simple timeline from engagement to wedding day.
blog_posts.content:
Bridal trials are one of my favorite parts of the wedding journey. They’re where we refine your vision, test products, and plan the timing for your wedding morning.

### Ideal timing
I generally recommend booking your bridal trial 8–12 weeks before your wedding date, with your wedding day booking secured several months earlier...

We’ll also talk through your bridal party numbers, travel logistics, and review my cancellation and rescheduling policy.

blog_posts.title: Natural Daytime Makeup for Professional Photoshoots
blog_posts.excerpt: Planning headshots or a branding shoot? Here’s how I approach soft, camera-ready makeup for daylight sessions.
blog_posts.content:
Daytime photoshoots call for makeup that looks fresh in person and polished on camera. In this article I break down my go-to approach for natural, camera-ready looks.

We’ll discuss base coverage levels, undertones that photograph well, and how to avoid flashback. This is the exact style many clients reference when they inquire via the contact form for Saturday morning photoshoots.

blog_posts.title: Top 5 Waterproof Mascaras for Tear-Proof Weddings
blog_posts.excerpt: Happy tears are guaranteed on a wedding day—smudged mascara doesn’t have to be.
blog_posts.content:
From emotional vows to heartfelt speeches, weddings are full of moments that bring on happy tears. Waterproof mascara is a must, especially for brides and bridal parties.

In this article I share my top five waterproof mascaras, how they perform in real wedding conditions, and tips for safe removal at the end of the night.

blog_posts.title: Soft Glam vs Full Glam: Which Is Right for Your Event?
blog_posts.excerpt: Not sure if you want soft glam or full glam? Here’s how I define each style with real portfolio examples.
blog_posts.content:
Clients often ask, "What’s the difference between soft glam and full glam?" In this post, I define both styles using real examples from my evening and party portfolio.

Soft glam emphasizes blended tones, flattering definition, and softer color stories, while full glam leans into bolder contrasts, heavier lashes, and more sculpted skin.

blog_posts.title: How to Prep Your Skin Before Your Makeup Appointment
blog_posts.excerpt: Proper skin prep can make or break how your makeup sits and lasts. Here’s my recommended routine for the night before and the morning of your appointment.
blog_posts.content:
Your makeup will only look as good as the skin underneath. Whether it’s for a bridal trial, photoshoot, or special occasion, a few simple steps can dramatically improve how your makeup wears.

This guide includes instructions for dry, normal, combination, and oily skin types, plus a downloadable prep checklist I send to all booked clients.

blog_posts.title: Bridal Party Makeup: Coordinating Looks for Your Bridesmaids
blog_posts.excerpt: Learn how to coordinate bridesmaid makeup so each person feels like themselves while still looking cohesive in photos.
blog_posts.content:
Coordinating makeup for a bridal party can feel overwhelming. In this article I share how I plan complementary looks for bridesmaids while still keeping the bride as the focal point.

We’ll talk through face charts, timing, and how my wedding rate calculator estimates costs for groups of four or more.

blog_posts.title: Makeup for Mature Skin: Techniques That Flatter
blog_posts.excerpt: Fine lines and texture are normal. Here’s how I adapt products and techniques to flatter mature skin.
blog_posts.content:
Mature skin deserves makeup that enhances, not hides. In this article I explain how I adjust product choices and techniques for clients with fine lines, texture, and changes in elasticity.

From foundation finishes to concealer placement and cream vs powder formulas, these are the methods I rely on for mothers of the bride, executives, and personal branding shoots.

blog_posts.title: Airbrush vs Traditional Foundation for Brides
blog_posts.excerpt: Not sure whether to choose airbrush or traditional foundation for your wedding day? I break down the pros and cons of each.
blog_posts.content:
Both airbrush and traditional foundation can look beautiful and last all day when applied correctly. The right choice depends on your skin type, coverage preferences, and the overall style of your wedding.

This article includes side-by-side comparisons from real bridal looks in my portfolio, plus my recommendations based on oily vs dry skin.

blog_posts.title: What to Expect from Your Bridal Trial Appointment
blog_posts.excerpt: From skincare prep to bringing inspiration photos, here’s how to get the most from your bridal trial.
blog_posts.content:
Your bridal trial is the perfect time to test ideas and finalize your wedding day look. In this article, I outline what happens step-by-step at a typical trial, including consultation, application, tweaks, and photos.

I also cover timing, what to wear, and how far in advance to schedule your trial relative to your wedding date.

blog_posts.title: Summer Wedding Makeup Tips to Beat the Heat
blog_posts.excerpt: Outdoor ceremony in July? Here’s how I keep makeup fresh and photo-ready in heat and humidity.
blog_posts.content: Summer weddings are beautiful—but hot weather can challenge even the best makeup. I share my favorite sweat-resistant products, setting techniques, and touch-up strategies for summer brides and bridal parties.

blog_posts.title: Choosing the Right False Lashes for Your Eye Shape
blog_posts.excerpt: From natural to dramatic, learn how I select lash styles that flatter different eye shapes.
blog_posts.content:
False lashes can transform a look—but only when they suit the wearer’s eye shape and comfort level. In this guide, I break down lash recommendations for almond, round, hooded, and monolid eyes.

Many of my bridal packages include lashes; this article explains what that means and how we decide on the day.

blog_posts.title: Sanitation Standards in a Professional Makeup Kit
blog_posts.excerpt: Here’s how I keep my kit hygienic and safe for every client, every time.
blog_posts.content:
Cleanliness is non-negotiable in my makeup practice. From brush washing routines to disposable applicators and product rotation, I follow strict sanitation standards.

In this article, I explain how I sanitize products between clients, manage cream products, and what you can expect to see on your appointment day.

blog_posts.title: Studio Policies: Deposits, Travel & Cancellations Explained
blog_posts.excerpt: A clear overview of my booking deposit, travel fees, minimums, and cancellation policy.
blog_posts.content:
To keep the booking process smooth and fair for everyone, I work with a clear set of studio policies.

In this post, I outline:
- When a non-refundable retainer is required.
- How travel within city limits is calculated.
- Minimum booking requirements for on-site weddings.
- Timelines and fees for rescheduling or cancellations.

You can also find a summarized version of the cancellation policy in the FAQ section.

blog_posts.title: Digital Makeup Gift Cards: The Perfect Last-Minute Present
blog_posts.excerpt: Need a thoughtful last-minute gift? Here’s how my digital gift cards work and what they can be redeemed for.
blog_posts.content:
Digital gift cards are a flexible way to treat someone to a makeup experience—whether it’s a lesson, special occasion makeup, or a bridal trial.

This article explains the available denominations (including $100), how to personalize the message, and how recipients can redeem cards when booking.

blog_posts.title: Smoky Eye Tutorial for Beginners
blog_posts.excerpt: A step-by-step guide to creating a soft smoky eye that works for every eye shape.
blog_posts.content:
This draft article will eventually walk through a simple smoky eye technique with photos and product suggestions.

It is not yet published and does not appear in the main blog listing.

faq_items.question: What is your cancellation policy?
faq_items.answer:
I understand that plans can change, and I aim to be as fair as possible while protecting reserved dates.

For individual appointments (trials, special occasion, photoshoots):
- Cancellations made 72 hours or more before the appointment: your non-refundable retainer remains on file and may be transferred once to a new date within 90 days, subject to availability.
- Cancellations made within 72 hours of the appointment: the retainer is forfeited and a cancellation fee of 50% of the remaining balance may apply.

For bridal bookings and group events:
- The non-refundable retainer paid at the time of booking holds your date and is not refundable under any circumstances.
- If you cancel your wedding or significantly reduce services more than 60 days before the event, any additional payments (beyond the retainer) will be refunded.
- Cancellations within 60 days of the event date may be subject to a cancellation fee of up to 75% of the remaining balance, as outlined in your contract.

Rescheduling due to illness or unforeseen circumstances is handled on a case-by-case basis. Please review your contract and contact me as soon as possible if you need to make changes.

faq_items.question: How do I book a bridal trial?
faq_items.answer:
You can request a bridal trial in two ways:

1. Visit the Services page and select the Bridal Makeup category. Choose the "Bridal Trial" service, view details, and use the "Book a Trial" section to select your preferred weekday date and time.
2. Or, use the Contact form to send an inquiry mentioning that you’d like to schedule a bridal trial.

Bridal trials are typically offered on weekdays and select Sundays. I recommend booking your trial 8–12 weeks before your wedding date.

faq_items.question: Do you travel on-site for weddings?
faq_items.answer:
Yes, I offer on-site services for weddings and group bookings.

Travel within city limits is often included in select bridal packages. For a la carte bookings, a flat travel fee may apply based on your location.

You can see whether "Travel within city limits" is included in a specific bridal package by checking the features list on the package details page.

faq_items.question: Is a deposit required to secure my date?
faq_items.answer:
Yes. A non-refundable retainer (often called a deposit) is required to secure all bridal bookings and most group appointments.

Your date is not reserved until the retainer is paid and your contract is signed. The retainer is applied toward your final balance on the event day.

Details about retainer amounts and due dates are outlined in your proposal and contract.

faq_items.question: What forms of payment do you accept?
faq_items.answer:
I accept major credit and debit cards, as well as select digital payment methods (such as Stripe-powered checkouts) for retainers and invoices.

Cash may be accepted for the remaining balance on the day of the appointment when arranged in advance. I do not accept personal checks.

Gift cards purchased through the Shop page can also be applied to eligible services.

faq_items.question: How far in advance should I book for my wedding?
faq_items.answer:
Most brides book 9–12 months in advance, especially for popular spring and fall dates.

If your wedding is sooner, it’s still worth inquiring—occasional weekday and off-peak dates may be available.

Use the Contact form or the Bridal Packages section on the Services page to check availability for your specific date.

faq_items.question: Do you offer group discounts for bridal parties?
faq_items.answer:
Yes, I offer tiered pricing for bridal parties based on the number of services booked.

You can use the Rate Calculator on the Pricing page to estimate costs for the bride and bridal party (for example, bride plus three bridesmaids). Once you have an estimate, click "Request this quote" to start an inquiry with those details pre-filled.

faq_items.question: What time of day should I schedule my bridal trial?
faq_items.answer:
I recommend scheduling your bridal trial during daylight hours if possible—late morning or early afternoon—so you can see how the makeup looks in natural light.

Weekday appointments typically have the most availability. Use the "Book a Trial" section on the Bridal Trial service page to view available weekday dates and times next month.

faq_items.question: Are false lashes included in bridal packages?
faq_items.answer:
Many of my bridal packages include false lashes for the bride and can add lashes for bridal party members.

On the Bridal Packages section of the Services page, check the features list for each package. Look for phrases like "False lashes included" to see whether they are built into the package or available as an add-on.

faq_items.question: Where is your studio located and is there parking?
faq_items.answer:
My studio is located in the city center, near public transit.

Paid street parking and a nearby parking garage are available. Full address and parking instructions are included in your booking confirmation and reminder emails.

faq_items.question: Do you offer vegan or cruelty-free makeup options?
faq_items.answer: Yes. My kit includes a range of vegan and cruelty-free products. If you have specific preferences or sensitivities, please note them in your inquiry or booking form so I can prepare suitable product options for your appointment.

faq_items.question: How long does makeup application take per person?
faq_items.answer:
For most events, allow 45–60 minutes per person.

Bridal makeup typically takes 60–75 minutes to allow for extra detail and any last-minute adjustments.

Exact timing will be outlined in your schedule once we confirm the number of services and start time.

faq_items.question: Can I bring my own makeup products?
faq_items.answer:
Yes, you are welcome to bring products you love or are sensitive about.

I’ll assess how they work with the rest of the look and may suggest a mix of your products and mine to achieve the best result. For hygiene reasons, I may decant or apply some items with disposable tools.

faq_items.question: Do you offer gift cards?
faq_items.answer:
Yes. Digital gift cards are available in various denominations, including $100, and can be purchased through the Shop page.

You can personalize the recipient name and message before adding the digital gift card to your cart. Gift cards can be applied toward eligible services such as makeup lessons, special occasion makeup, or bridal trials.

services.name: 1:1 Makeup Lesson (90 Minutes)
services.shortDescription: Personalized makeup lesson focused on your everyday routine or a specific look.
services.description: A private 90-minute lesson to refresh your routine or master a specific look like a red lip or soft smokey eye. We work with your existing products and fill in gaps with recommendations from my kit.

services.name: False Lash Application Only
services.shortDescription: Standalone lash application using strip lashes.
services.description: Already have your makeup done but want a little extra definition? This quick service includes trimming and applying strip lashes using professional adhesive.
features: Perfect for events when you just need lashes
